arguments.name: Apple — Internal
arguments.description: Internal workspace for Apple employees: company news, leadership communications, people resources, product & R&D updates, and mandatory policies.
arguments.welcome_message: Welcome to Apple — Internal. This hub centralizes company news, leadership communications, people resources, product & R&D updates, and essential policies for all Apple employees worldwide.

arguments.name: Apple Newsroom
arguments.description: Apple Newsroom publishes official company announcements, product launch recaps, and financial summaries. Use this channel for items that should be visible to everyone — press releases, quarterly results, and major product milestones.

arguments.name: Leadership & Strategy
arguments.description: Leadership & Strategy contains CEO letters, executive briefings, and strategic roadmaps intended for leaders and all employees. It is the place for measured, company‑level communications and reflections from the executive team.

arguments.name: Product & R&D
arguments.description: Product & R&D shares release retrospectives, engineering highlights, and cross‑team research updates — including Apple Silicon, Vision Pro, and Apple Intelligence workstreams.

frequent_questions.question: How can I nominate a paper or demo for internal showcase?
frequent_questions.answer: Submit a short summary and contact details to the R&D showcase mailbox; engineering managers curate items monthly.

frequent_questions.question: Where are product spec archives stored?
frequent_questions.answer: Product specs and technical journals are on internal Pages linked from this channel; use the product search to find the latest version.

frequent_questions.question: Who to contact for developer tooling questions?
frequent_questions.answer: Reach out to the Developer Tools team via the internal directory or post to the designated channel on internal messaging.

arguments.name: People & Inclusion
arguments.description: People & Inclusion provides benefits updates, parental leave info, ERG spotlights (Black@Apple, Pride@Apple, Women@Apple, Veterans@Apple, Accessibility@Apple), and career development resources.

frequent_questions.question: How do I enroll in parental leave?
frequent_questions.answer: Contact HR at hr@apple.com and follow the Parental Leave page for required forms and timelines.

frequent_questions.question: How are RSU grants administered?
frequent_questions.answer: Grant schedules and tax guidance are posted on the Compensation page; contact Total Rewards for case‑specific questions.

frequent_questions.question: How do I join an ERG?
frequent_questions.answer: ERG membership is self‑service via the People portal — find group pages under People & Inclusion.

frequent_questions.question: Where can I find training and learning credits?
frequent_questions.answer: Learning & Development manages tuition and course credits on the Pages section; request approval through your manager.

frequent_questions.question: Who to contact for workplace accommodations?
frequent_questions.answer: Accessibility@Apple coordinates accommodations; raise requests through HR or the Accessibility form on the Pages tab.

arguments.name: Retail & Field Operations
arguments.description: Retail & Field Operations supports Apple Store teams with playbooks, staffing updates, and retail metrics. Content includes product launch readiness guides and in‑store training schedules.

frequent_questions.question: How do stores receive launch stock schedules?
frequent_questions.answer: Launch logistics are posted to the Pages tab and distributed via the Retail Ops distribution list.

frequent_questions.question: Who manages Genius Bar scheduling?
frequent_questions.answer: Store managers use the scheduling tool; escalation to regional Ops if there are access issues.

frequent_questions.question: Where to find training modules for new devices?
frequent_questions.answer: Training modules and certification paths are in the Pages > Training library.

frequent_questions.question: What is the retail discount policy?
frequent_questions.answer: Employee purchase policies are listed on the People & Inclusion pages and replicated here for convenience.

arguments.name: Supply Chain & Operations
arguments.description: Supply Chain & Operations publishes supplier responsibility reports, manufacturing updates (including India and Vietnam expansion), and sustainability metrics tied to the 2030 carbon goals.

frequent_questions.question: Where is the Supplier Responsibility Report?
frequent_questions.answer: The latest report is linked on the Pages tab with downloadable assets and contact info for supplier inquiries.

frequent_questions.question: How do we report a supplier compliance concern?
frequent_questions.answer: Use the supplier hotline and internal reporting form; details are on the Pages tab.

frequent_questions.question: Who leads regional manufacturing relations?
frequent_questions.answer: Regional Ops leads are listed on the channel Pages with contact emails.

frequent_questions.question: How is progress toward 2030 tracked?
frequent_questions.answer: Progress dashboards are maintained by ESG teams and linked from this channel.

arguments.name: Legal, Privacy & Compliance
arguments.description: Legal, Privacy & Compliance issues company‑wide legal notices, regulatory updates, required policy acknowledgements, and privacy guidance. Mandatory acknowledgements will be posted here with read‑confirmation requirements.

frequent_questions.question: Which regulators are we actively engaging with?
frequent_questions.answer: Teams should consult Legal; public matters include DOJ antitrust and EU regulators — contact legal@apple.com for guidance.

frequent_questions.question: How do I complete required policy acknowledgements?
frequent_questions.answer: Policy posts will include a "Mark as read" button; mandatory items require Read Confirmation.

frequent_questions.question: Who to contact about data subject requests?
frequent_questions.answer: Privacy@apple.com coordinates DSARs; process documentation is on Pages.

frequent_questions.question: Where are export control resources?
frequent_questions.answer: Export control guidance and forms are on the Pages tab; escalate to Legal for complex cases.

frequent_questions.question: What to do if I suspect a compliance breach?
frequent_questions.answer: Report immediately to legal@apple.com and the compliance hotline; instructions on the channel Pages.

arguments.name: IT, Security & Developer Tools
arguments.description: IT, Security & Developer Tools posts scheduled maintenance, security patches, SSO notices, and developer tooling updates. Critical security advisories require immediate attention and may require read confirmation.

frequent_questions.question: How do I report a suspected phishing email?
frequent_questions.answer: Use the phishing report button in mail and notify security@apple.com; guidance is on the Pages tab.

frequent_questions.question: What is the SSO outage escalation path?
frequent_questions.answer: The incident runbook and on‑call contacts are on the Pages tab.

frequent_questions.question: How to request access to developer tools?
frequent_questions.answer: Submit an access request through the Developer Tools Page; approvals follow team policy.

frequent_questions.question: Where to find VPN and remote access instructions?
frequent_questions.answer: Step‑by‑step guides with screenshots are available on Pages.

frequent_questions.question: Are cameras or audio devices supported for Vision Pro dev?
frequent_questions.answer: Hardware guidelines and provisioning procedures are on the Product & R&D Pages; contact Platform Support for provisioning.

arguments.title: Q4 FY2024 Results: Financial Highlights and Year in Review
arguments.body:
Q4 FY2024 Results: Financial Highlights

Today we published our financial results for fiscal year 2024. For the full fiscal year, Apple reported $391.0 billion in revenue with an operating income of $123.2 billion and a net income of $93.7 billion. These results reflect the strength of our product and services portfolio and the continued resilience of our global business.

Segment performance

  • iPhone: The iPhone segment remains our largest contributor, representing roughly 52% of total revenue.
  • Services: Services continue to grow and now represent approximately 26% of revenue, contributing around $96 billion annually.
  • Mac & iPad: Mac revenue is smaller by proportion but remains strategically important for our platform; iPad contributes steadily to our product mix.

What this means for teams

These results validate our investments in hardware, software, and services. Teams across engineering, retail, and services should continue to focus on quality, reliability, and privacy as we move into FY2025.

“We closed the year strong across every product line.” — Tim Cook

Read the full report and investor materials at investor.apple.com.

arguments.title: iPhone 16 Launch Recap and Customer Reception
arguments.body:
iPhone 16 Launch Recap

The iPhone 16 family launched in September 2024, introducing the Camera Control button and the A18 chip across the lineup. Store teams and marketing collaborated closely to deliver a premium customer experience globally.

Early reception

Customer feedback highlights improved imaging controls and battery life on the Pro models. Operations teams reported smooth logistics in most regions; localized challenges were addressed by regional Ops teams.

Support and resources

  • Retail playbooks and demo kit instructions are linked in Retail & Field Operations.
  • Engineering notes and benchmarks are available in Product & R&D.

For more details, consult product pages on the internal Product & R&D channel.

arguments.title: Vision Pro — First-Year Product Retrospective
arguments.body:
Vision Pro — Year One Retrospective

Since the Vision Pro launch in January 2024, the product and ecosystem have matured with updates to visionOS and a growing developer community. This retrospective reflects on product learnings and developer feedback from the first year.

Software and developer growth

visionOS 2 delivered platform improvements; developer uptake continues as studios and independent creators explore spatial experiences.

Customer experience and distribution

Availability in select markets focused on early adopters, with learnings informing future hardware and retail strategies.

For developer resources, see Product & R&D channel pages.

arguments.title: Services Growth and App Store Ecosystem Update
arguments.body:
Services Growth and App Store Update

Services now represent approximately 26% of Apple revenue, with annual contribution near $96 billion. The App Store continues to be a key platform for creators and developers globally.

Developer initiatives

We continue to expand developer tools, monetization options, and engagement programs to support a flourishing App Store ecosystem.

Customer experience

Investments in discoverability and editorial programming aim to improve customer satisfaction and long-term retention.

arguments.title: A Letter from the CEO: Looking Ahead to FY2025
arguments.body:
Letter from the CEO

Dear colleagues,

As we enter FY2025, our focus remains on innovation, operational excellence, and privacy-first experiences. Thank you for your dedication — together we will continue to deliver products that enrich people's lives.

— Tim Cook

arguments.title: Apple Silicon: Research Notes and Roadmap
arguments.body:
Apple Silicon: Research Notes

Our teams continue to optimize performance-per-watt and integrate machine learning accelerators across SoC designs. This page summarizes recent chip milestones and upcoming focus areas.

Roadmap highlights

  • Continued performance uplifts across CPU and GPU subsystems
  • Expanded NPU capabilities to accelerate on-device intelligence
  • Collaborations with hardware teams for thermal design improvements

For detailed benchmarks, see linked docs in the Product & R&D Pages section.

arguments.title: Parental Leave Policy Update — 2025 Guidelines
arguments.body:
Parental Leave Policy Update

Effective Jan 1, 2025, Apple updates parental leave entitlements to provide greater flexibility and support. These changes aim to better support new parents and caregivers.

Key changes

  • Increased paid leave duration for primary caregivers
  • Flexible return-to-work options and phased re-entry
  • Expanded support resources via People & Inclusion

Contact HR at hr@apple.com for individual cases and forms.

arguments.title: Retail Launch Playbook: iPhone 16 — Key Steps for Stores
arguments.body:
Retail Launch Playbook: iPhone 16

This playbook outlines store‑level steps for a smooth iPhone 16 launch: merchandising, demo kit checklists, staffing plans, and troubleshooting common issues.

Before launch

  • Verify demo kits are fully charged and updated
  • Check in with regional Ops for stock allocations
  • Train floor staff on new Camera Control features

Refer to the Pages section for downloadable checklists and scheduling templates.

arguments.title: 2030 Sustainability Progress Update
arguments.body:
2030 Sustainability Progress Update

We report progress toward our 2030 climate goals, including reductions in manufacturing emissions and supplier engagement programs. This update includes key metrics and next steps for teams driving sustainability initiatives.

Highlights

  • Progress on supplier renewables adoption
  • Improvements in packaging and materials
  • Case studies of regional manufacturing partners

For dashboards and datasets, see channel Pages.

arguments.title: Mandatory Export Compliance Acknowledgement
arguments.body:
Export Compliance: Mandatory Acknowledgement

All employees working with regulated technologies must acknowledge updated export control policies. This post requires read confirmation.

Please read the policy and mark as acknowledged by the deadline.
